title: HTTP/1.1 Response Serialization, Field Order, Framing, and Writes · Language Lens: Output Framing Selection
summary: A writer emits content before headers, injects unchecked field values, chooses conflicting framing, assumes one Write completes, and cannot prove exact transmitted bytes. Planned artifact: Add and verify the output framing selection increment in canonical bounded HTTP/1.1 response serializer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose no-content, fixed-length, chunked, or close-delimited output from method, status, known length, streaming, version, and reuse policy.
Reject CR, LF, NUL, controls, invalid names, and unsafe values before serializing any response field.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Output Framing Selection · Theory Output Framing Selection
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 2381 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to select one output framing mode while retaining partial-write and field-validation evidence; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then change certificate identity while ALPN stays valid and reject the connection. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Output Framing Selection contract for the supplied data.
